exemplar.title: To what extent does social media contribute to the polarization of abortion rights, and how does this impact advocacy efforts, public opinion, and policy development?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does social media contribute to the polarization of abortion rights, and how does this impact advocacy efforts, public opinion, and policy development?

strengths: Logical sequence of headings and inclusion of citations
weaknesses: Frequent abrupt section breaks and mid-sentence truncations interrupt flow
Repetition and formatting inconsistencies impede coherence
Numerous citation style errors and misattributions reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the report is readable, pervasive structural and formatting lapsesâincluding incomplete sentences, abrupt transitions, and inconsistent referencingâsignificantly hinder clarity and cohesion. These recurring issues place the work in the lowest band for Communication, meriting a score of 1 out of 3.

strengths: Issue clearly identified with precise context and a focused research question
Importance of the project justified with concrete links to human rights and state legitimacy
Engagement activities explicitly linked to elements of the research question
weaknesses: Minor repetition and occasional wording issues limit overall sophistication
Depth of empirical support for project importance could be strengthened
Justification of activity relevance, while convincing, lacks exhaustive empirical grounding
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place the student firmly in the top band (3â4) across all three strands of Explanation & Justification. The issue is clearly stated and contextualized, the projectâs importance is well justified, and the relevance of engagement activities is convincingly linked to the research question. Occasional repetition and lack of empirical depth prevent the highest mark, so a score of 3 out of 4 accurately reflects a strong but not fully exceptional performance.

strengths: Evidence of forethought in selecting interviews and a survey
Engagement activities chosen logically to address different facets of the issue
Some integration of research findings into the analysis narrative
weaknesses: No detailed timeline or resource planning for engagements
Logistical details (dates, sequencing, contingencies) are absent
Integration of research into subsequent engagement design is only occasional
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Process strands land in the midâband (2) range. The plan is basic but appropriate, with logical engagement choices and some researchâengagement linkage. However, missing timelines, resource considerations, and explicit cyclical integration prevent a topâband score. A score of 2 out of 3 reflects this solid yet underdeveloped planning.

strengths: Clear, balanced politicalâissue analysis exploring echo chambers, algorithms, and power dynamics
Purposeful application of core Global Politics concepts such as soft power and human rights
Synthesis of stakeholder perspectives into a coherent comparative discussion
weaknesses: Limited critical interrogation of source credibility and methodology
Analysis rarely reaches multi-layered causality or deep counter-argument
Theoretical references sometimes remain descriptive rather than fully integrated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments identify a lowerâband 3â4 performance: concepts are applied accurately, analysis is clear and balanced, and stakeholder views are woven together. Yet the work stops short of the highest bandâs depth and critical interrogation. A holistic score of 3 out of 8 (reflecting 3â4 band) captures the solid but not exceptional level of analysis and synthesis.

strengths: Partial evaluation of strengths and limitations of research and engagement
Adequate reflection on lessons learned and future implications
weaknesses: Awareness of personal bias is minimal and unexplored
Evaluation lacks systematic criteria or methodological critique
Reflective insight, while present, is not deeply critical or comprehensive
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the student demonstrates some evaluation and reflective learning, the unacknowledged personal bias and absence of systematic evaluative criteria limit the depth. Strand ratings of 3, 1, and 3 translate into a midâband holistic score of 2 out of 6, reflecting partial evaluation and surfaceâlevel reflection.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student clearly names the issueââpolarization and politicization of reproductive rights on digital platformsââand immediately restates it through a precise research question. Background on social-media dynamics, abortion as a human-rights debate, and the concepts of liberty and soft power provide sufficient context for a general reader. Minor repetition and occasional wording issues prevent the fullest sophistication, but the explanation is unmistakably clear and contextualised, meriting the lower end of the top band.

There is evidence of forethoughtâidentification of two interviews and a survey and some cited academic literatureâbut no timeline, selection criteria, or explanation of how sources were located beyond convenience. The plan is basic and mostly appropriate rather than fully structured, fitting the 2-mark descriptor.

Engagement activities are chosen logically, but logistical detail (dates, sequencing, contingency arrangements) is absent. Feasibility is implied rather than demonstrated, so the strand corresponds to a partial plan worth 2 marks.

Core Global Politics ideasâsoft power, legitimacy, liberty, globalization, human rightsâare applied consistently to explain observations. Although some definitions are textbook-like and not always critically interrogated, usage is mostly accurate and purposeful, aligning with adequate application in the 3-4 band; 3 reflects minor lapses.

The narrative shows that findings from each engagement are used to shape discussion (e.g., interview insights quoted in analysis), yet there is no cyclical refinement or explicit statement that research informed subsequent questions. The linkage is occasional and functional, matching the middle band.

Each planned activity (NGO interview, public poll, party interview) is explicitly tied to elements of the research questionâadvocacy, public opinion, policy. The write-up explains how each will illuminate soft-power use, algorithmic effects, or policy influence. Links are convincing though not exhaustively justified, placing the work firmly in the 3-4 band at a score of 3.

Language is generally clear and grammatical, though run-on sentences, mid-paragraph heading insertions, and occasional vocabulary misuse ("incredibility") obscure meaning intermittently. These minor issues reduce coherence to the 2-mark level.

The student analyses misinformation, echo chambers, and algorithms, relating them to stakeholder power and human-rights impacts. Discussion moves beyond description but seldom reaches multi-layered causality or counter-argument depth. This satisfies clear analysis at the lower end of the 3-4 band.

The report notes exposure to hostile content and acknowledges that algorithms can reinforce bias, yet the student never explicitly examines personal positionality or how their own beliefs may have shaped the work. Minimal recognition places this in the 1-2 band at the lower score.

The piece follows a logical sequence with headings, appendix, and citations; however, some repetition and abrupt section breaks (e.g., page fragments) interrupt flow. This corresponds to a âbasic structure; some confusionâ descriptor for 2 marks.

The conclusion comments on strengths (reach, empowerment) and limitations (misinformation, hostility) of both research and engagement, but evaluation is general and lacks systematic criteria or methodological critique. This fits âpartial evaluationâ at 3 marks.

The student justifies importance by linking the debate to saving lives, protecting human rights, and shaping state legitimacy, then explains why personal interest and course concepts make the engagement suitable. Reasons are concrete and specific rather than generic, although they are not explored in depth or with empirical evidence; hence a solid but not exceptional performance within the top band.

Perspectives from an NGO, a political party member, and the public are brought together and compared (e.g., all acknowledge polarization but differ on policy impact). Links are made, though integration remains descriptive in places. This meets partial synthesis expectations for 3 marks.

The closing paragraph discusses lessons learned about the dual nature of social media and hints at future regulatory needs, showing adequate but not deeply critical reflection. Insights are present but not expansive, warranting 3 marks.

annotations.comment: The student explicitly identifies the political issue with precision, providing clear context for the engagement activity. This clarity sets a strong foundation for the research question.
The research question is well-aligned with the issue and clearly articulated, though splitting it across lines affects readability; consider consolidating into one sentence for coherence.
This introductory paragraph effectively situates the issue within the broader context of global politics, demonstrating robust explanation and justification of relevance.

The student justifies the projectâs importance with concrete reasons, linking societal impact and human rights; however, empirical evidence to support these claims would strengthen the justification.
While engagement activities are logically chosen, the plan lacks specific timelines and resource considerations to demonstrate feasibility, limiting the planningâs depth.
The interview outcome is described clearly, but the student should detail how interview questions were developed from research to demonstrate integration between research and engagement.
The poll engagement provides useful insights into algorithmic polarization, yet lacks information on sample size and demographic composition, weakening the reliability of the findings.
âAfterward, I interviewed Manjeet Sahotaâ¦â ends mid-sentence; ensure complete sentences to maintain coherence and fully convey findings from the political perspective.
The student effectively synthesizes interview, poll, and literature insights, but does not critically assess the credibility and potential bias of quoted sources, limiting analytical depth.
The sentence structure in the poll results explanation is unclear (âthose who saw the is subseâ¦â). Clarify and correct this to ensure the data interpretation is understandable.
The use of group polarization theory demonstrates purposeful application of course concepts, strengthening the analysis with theoretical grounding.
Citing Pagoto et al. effectively illustrates real-world implications, yet the student could deepen analysis by critiquing the methodology of this study to evaluate its applicability.
The explanation of echo chambers and algorithm effects is thorough and integrates theoretical and empirical sources, reflecting strong synthesis of stakeholder perspectives.
Consider explaining how the transition between negative and positive cases of social media influence ties back to your research question to enhance coherence.
The quantitative poll data (63.3% agreement) adds credibility, but missing sample size and margin of error decrease the robustness of this evidence.
Strong connection to secondary literature (Shirky, Raju) demonstrates effective engagement with external sources to support arguments.
The student synthesizes interview and poll insights well but should explicitly reflect on how these findings informed subsequent research questions to complete the researchâengagement cycle.
Quoting statistics on misinformation speed adds authority, but the source attribution to 'MIT researchers' is inaccurate given the Internet Society citation; verify and correct attribution.
The discussion on globalization and NGO empowerment provides insightful reflection on digital engagement, indicating depth in analysis and synthesis.
Awareness of personal bias is minimal; the student should articulate how their own values influenced research design and interpretation of results.
The conclusion effectively summarizes findings across engagements but repeats earlier language; consider more concise synthesis for reader clarity.
Reflection on future policy implications demonstrates reflective insight, but the student could deepen evaluation by proposing specific regulatory measures.
Awareness of personal bias is minimal here; the student should explicitly examine how their stance on reproductive rights shaped interpretation and engagement choices.
The works cited list is comprehensive, but citation style is inconsistent (e.g., missing italics, variation in access date formats); standardize formatting.
Journal article citation correctly includes a DOI; however, order of authors and punctuation should follow a consistent style guide.
The NPR source entry lacks retrieval date and proper title formatting; ensure all entries are complete and uniformly styled.
Secondary references are current and relevant, demonstrating strong engagement with scholarly literature to support analysis.
âSocial Media: The Influenceâ¦â citation lacks author name and date placement; revisit entry to provide complete bibliographic details.
Sunsteinâs group polarization article is foundational but could be more explicitly integrated in the main text to show how this theory informs the research question.

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue not identified or irrelevant
2. No meaningful explanation

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: No discussion of why the project matters

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities absent or unrelated to issue

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue named but explanation vague / superficial
2. Limited or missing context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability mentioned but weak or generic

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities listed; relevance implied or weakly linked

strands.name: Political issue identification & clarity
strands.content:
1. Issue explicitly stated
2. Clear, precise explanation with relevant context

strands.name: Project importance & suitability
strands.content: Importance / suitability clearly justified with specific reasons

strands.name: Relevance of engagement activities
strands.content: Activities fully explained and convincingly linked to the issue

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: No link between research and engagement

strands.name: Engagement planning
strands.content: Activities planned but timing / logistics patchy

strands.name: Integration of research â engagement
strands.content: Research consistently informs engagement; findings feed back into further inquiry

strands.name: Depth of political-issue analysis
strands.content: Clear, balanced analysis; key factors explored

strands.name: Synthesis of stakeholder perspectives
strands.content: Effective synthesis; perspectives woven into coherent argument

strands.name: Awareness of personal bias
strands.content: Biases explored; impact on work examined

strands.name: Organization & structure
strands.content: Logical structure; mostly easy to follow
